bio.title: Junior Web Developer
bio.subtitle: My goal is to create aesthetically pleasing and functional solutions through code and design. With a growing portfolio of successful projects, I am committed to continuously advancing my skills and knowledge. I am eager to bring my expertise and passion to a collaborative team, where I can contribute effectively, learn from others, and grow alongside talented professionals.

sectionOne.title: About me
texts: Hello! I'm Benja, a full-stack developer who loves technology and design. I'm Argentine, I live in Buenos Aires, and I'm 24 years old. If you took the time to explore and create your account, I sincerely appreciate it. Now I can spam your email until you hire me… just kidding 👀. You can now tweet whatever you want and interact with what I've built. Want to say something nice about my projects? I'd really appreciate it. This small community holds a special place in my heart.
I'm 24 years young, this journey into coding has been short and sweet, and I'd love to guide you through it. I think I've got room to blab about me first though, right?

I'm not your typical nerd. I tried university for (very) long two years and realized that academic suffering wasn't for me. Or maybe I just chose the wrong path? As a kid, I wanted to be a biochemist. But after two years at UBA, I understood that I was NOT going to be the next up and coming Walter White… more like down and retreating am I right? No? Hello?

Perhaps it was due to a lack of interest or the fact that, shortly after graduating from high school, I entered the most stagnant period of my life—six years, to be exact. During this time, I worked as an accounting assistant in my grandfather's office, performing repetitive and monotonous tasks. I didn't enjoy it, but it provided me with financial stability and allowed me to support my hobbies.

What did I learn from this experience? That I strongly dislike routine and monotony. I thrive in a dynamic and engaging environment, one that constantly challenges me, pushes me to learn new things, and encourages my continuous growth. I seek a workplace where I can advance and develop professionally—something my grandfather's company would not have been able to offer me.

Oh yeah, so this is me. In my free time, I like to make electronic music. Yes, scoliosis and shoulder pain till death for me thanks. Specifically, for those who know the scene (real ones, word), I make peak-time techno, a genre that is quite popular nowadays in the world — the one outside my room I've been told about. So yeah, I'm quite the stay-at-home nerd, but I promise I have friends and a girlfriend (at least as of now). But if I didn't have any loved ones you just know I would be straight crushing it in Assembly by now.
So anyways, the pictures below are from a photoshoot I invested in because you can't be a techno artist without going for the casual, kinda-gothic, and cringy Insta feed.

texts.randomTalent: Picking up things with my feet. Don't ask me how I discovered it, but it's a talent that has come in handy on more than one occasion.
texts.name: Benjamin Henley Saubidet
texts.favouriteThing: Spamming W's in the group chat.
texts.leastFavouriteThing: Getting hate for spamming W's in the group chat.
texts.hobbies: Making it rain, producing fresh beatz, Working out, Rocking out, meditation, park-time with the doggies (not the one in the pic), clubbing, DJ'ing in cool clubs, binge watching youtube shorts until unconscious, PARKOUR, learning new things, traveling (still planning my world tour) and connecting with people.
texts.feats: I love connecting people. I've made friends during wild nights in Buenos Aires and introduced them to each other. I enjoy communication, even tough conversations. I love pushing boundaries, learning new skills, and perfecting old ones. My room walls are painted from quarantine days. I'm super Argentine, so I love asados and mate. But don't talk about our economy.
texts.coffee: Im having two coffees a day, don't judge. I'm a coffee lover, not a coffee snob... but I havo to admit I like starbucks. I also drink mate, a traditional Argentine drink.

projectArray.title: E-commerce Backend
projectArray.description: Check out my e-commerce backend, complete with comprehensive Postman documentation.

projectArray.title: Ibijet Rent
projectArray.description: Looking to have fun in Ibiza? I recommend renting a jetski to explore the beautiful sights this island has to offer. I designed a powerful algorithm that checks the availability of both guides and jetskis, along with a robust backend using Firebase. This ensures you can easily rent a jetski and enjoy a smooth experience.

projectArray.title: Crossup Challenge
projectArray.description: While actively job hunting, I interviewed with Crossup, a company specializing in sales on MiTienda Nube. Unfortunately, I wasn't selected, but I gained the experience of coding an aesthetically pleasing store that has enhanced my portfolio.

projectArray.title: Post Cosecha
projectArray.description: Post Cosecha was one of those projects undertaken solo. With little information from the client and much room for imagination, this freedom allowed me to express myself and create a unique style. The only expense incurred by the client was a video for the cover.

projectArray.title: Expo San Juan
projectArray.description: I've had a great experience working with Panorama Minero, a company at the forefront of the mining industry. They have been pleased to work with me on multiple occasions. This time, I improved the UI by internationalizing their San Juan Expo page, making it accessible in both English and Spanish. Additionally, I collaborated with a design team to add new sections to the page.

projectArray.title: Women In Mining
projectArray.description: International Women in Mining (IWiM) is an international organization run by volunteers committed to the advancement of women in the mining sector. I was happy to develop this page dedicated to the inclusion and visibility of the female workforce.

projectArray.title: Gold Silver And Copper
projectArray.description: I had the privilege of contributing to the creation of Panorama Minero's Gold, Silver, and Copper page. This project involved extensive collaboration to develop a comprehensive and visually appealing resource for users interested in these key mining sectors. I internationalized the page, making it accessible in both English and Spanish, which broadened its reach to a wider audience. Additionally, I optimized the user interface to ensure a seamless and engaging experience, integrating intuitive navigation and interactive elements. My work improved the page's usability and accessibility, adding significant value to Panorama Minero by attracting a more diverse and global audience.

projectArray.title: Buenos Aires Vision
projectArray.description: My first solo project was a great success. I designed a web page for Buenos Aires Vision, a tourism company known for providing the best selection of tours in Buenos Aires. Their offerings range from exquisite dining experiences to river explorations, ensuring a memorable stay for visitors. My role involved showcasing these tours in an elegant and appealing manner, which effectively attracted clients and increased the company's revenue.

techStack.title: Tech Stack
techStack.description: I work with a modern and efficient tech stack, combining front-end, back-end, databases, and cloud technologies to build scalable applications.

softSkills.title: Soft Skills
softSkills.description: Every project is an opportunity to grow, innovate, and refine my expertise. Below is a glimpse into the skills that shape my work and drive my passion for technology.

texts.title: Technical Skills
texts.description: I'm a full-stack developer specializing in React, Next.js, and Nest.js with GraphQL and Apollo Client. I work with PostgreSQL, Firebase Firestore, and enjoy styling with Tailwind CSS. I have experience with Docker for scalable deployments. Currently, I'm learning React Native and Swift for native app development. I'm also digging into AWS for cloud services and serverless architecture.

texts.title: Bilingual Experience & UI/UX
texts.description: Fun fact: my father is English, so I am bilingual. These skills in English and Spanish have allowed me to collaborate efficiently in diverse teams, and with numerous clients. improving internationalization, UI/UX, and accessibility.

texts.title: Project Collaboration
texts.description: I enjoy working toward a team vision. At ibijetrent, I collaborated with a frontend developer to integrate complex features into a jet ski reservation platform, delivering a solution that exceeded expectations. At Encender, I work with a team of developers to build and integrate iframes into the main Salesforce site.

texts.title: Database & API Experience
texts.description: I've built and optimized SQL (PostgreSQL) and NoSQL (Firebase) databases for scalable applications, implementing efficient CRUD operations and designing GraphQL APIs with Apollo Client for seamless data fetching. This web page's backend is built with Node.js and Nest.js, with tweet data retrieved from a PostgreSQL database. I also containerized the application using Docker, ensuring a consistent and portable development environment.

texts.title: Problem-Solving
texts.description: I enjoy breaking down complex problems and applying design principles and best development practices to find efficient solutions. Analyzing a problem from multiple angles allows me to deliver innovative and well-structured results. When designing a project's architecture, I prioritize scalability, maintainability, and performance to ensure long-term efficiency. If this means learning a new technology, to update existing infrastructure so be it.

texts.title: Creativity & Innovation
texts.description: In my programming career, creativity and innovation are at the core of everything I build. I constantly explore new technologies and frameworks, blending them in unique ways to develop efficient and visually engaging solutions. Whether designing complex algorithms or intuitive user interfaces, I focus on thinking outside the box to solve problems and enhance user experiences. I enjoy experimenting with fresh ideas, pushing boundaries, and turning challenges into opportunities for growth. This mindset allows me to create impactful projects that not only meet technical requirements but also bring a creative spark to the digital space.

texts.title: Adaptability & Continuous Learning
texts.description: I'm always exploring new technologies and methodologies to enhance my skills and stay ahead of industry trends. I'm currently learning about native app and exploring new frameworks, together with libraries to improve both my frontend and backend coding capabilities.

texts.title: Communication & Teamwork
texts.description: I have experience collaborating with multidisciplinary teams, facilitating effective communication, and ensuring smooth project progress. I'm comfortable working with clients to understand their needs and deliver solutions that meet their expectations.

texts.title: Time Management & Organization
texts.description: I excel at managing multiple tasks and prioritizing effectively to ensure timely, high-quality project deliveries. I have a strong work ethic and believe that the best way to learn is through hands-on experience—this mindset has shaped my programming journey. My first freelance projects taught me the importance of deadlines and delivering quality work on time. At Encender, I am adapting to a new way of working, fast-paced environment, working with multiple clients and projects, which has strengthened my ability to work dynamically and efficiently.

education.description: Completed APX course on Full Stack Development and Udemy courses on Mobile Development

careerMilestone.title: Career Milestone
careerMilestone.description: Landed my first company job at Encender after months of hard work and dedication
